methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Proxima Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised €145M to date. The valuation universe for nuclear energy remains sparse: three public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. This limited set reflects the early-stage, capital-intensive nature of the sector.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints within the nuclear energy cohort. Only one same-stage comparable and one adjacent-stage peer were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for meaningful triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would risk material distortion given the sector's unique economics and development timelines. As a result, no quantitative valuation range can be derived from the available comparable data.

Key uncertainties constraining valuation work include the sparse public market liquidity for nuclear technology companies, wide dispersion in development stage and technology maturity across private peers, and limited precedent for late-stage funding rounds in this subsector. Any valuation will require either: (1) expansion of the comp set via deeper private market intelligence on funding rounds for X-Energy, Kairos Power, or TerraPower; or (2) scenario-based approaches grounded in levelized cost of energy, regulatory pathway risk, and capital intensity benchmarks specific to Proxima's fusion technology approach.
